notes: Data mining rebranded as machine learning and predictive analytics; now dominant enterprise discipline
Decision trees remain widely used; Random Forest and Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) are major extensions
Neural networks became deep learning; transformer models and LLMs are current dominant form (2023+)
Association rules (Apriori algorithm) still used in recommendation systems and market basket analysis
MMP evolved; modern distributed computing (Spark, Ray) is the successor architecture
Parallelism became standard in ML training; GPU computing (NVIDIA CUDA) now primary parallel compute for ML
Commercial data mining platforms evolved into AutoML and cloud ML services (AWS SageMaker, Azure ML, GCP Vertex)
Predictive analytics became standard business practice across financial services, retail, telecom
K-means and other clustering algorithms remain widely used; deep learning-based clustering extended
Customer behavior/churn analysis became standard; powers subscription businesses and e-commerce globally
